engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo M48.81 engine - petrol 2.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented on early-production M48.81 units, typically 60k-90k miles.
Turbocharger oil seal failure - boost pressure loss and smoke from exhaust on higher-mileage examples.

cards.altText: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo M48.92 engine - petrol 4.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure - loss of power and rough running, commonly reported 50k-80k miles.
Coolant system leaks - water pump and thermostat housing failures leading to overheating risk.

cards.altText: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo M48.94 engine - hybrid 4.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Panamera Sport Turismo Turbo S E-Hybrid (971) - 2017-2020
Panamera Sport Turismo Turbo S E-Hybrid facelift (971.2) - 2021-2024
knownFailures: Hybrid battery degradation - reduced electric-only range and performance, typically after 8-10 years.
ECU software compatibility issues - post-replacement coding required for hybrid system integration.
cards.specs: Plug-in Hybrid · 4.0L · 680hp combined · Twin-Turbocharged + Electric Motor
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £8,500-£15,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable M48.94 units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.9L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level V6 for the Sport Turismo, delivering refined performance with twin-turbo efficiency across 4 and 4S variants.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented on early-production units, typically 60k-90k miles.
Turbocharger oil seal degradation - smoke from exhaust and boost loss on higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.80 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 2.9L Petrol
entries.history: High-output V6 powering the 4S, offering balanced performance and efficiency for the premium shooting brake.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - most searched failure for M48.81, preventative replacement recommended.
High-pressure fuel pump failure - rough running and loss of power, commonly 50k-80k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.81 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Foundation V8 for GTS variants, delivering strong torque and refined power delivery for the Sport Turismo body.
commonFailures: Coolant system leaks - water pump and thermostat housing failures leading to overheating risk.
Valve cover gasket deterioration - oil leaks onto exhaust components, common after 70k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.90 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Upgraded V8 for GTS models, featuring enhanced mapping and exhaust for the performance-oriented Sport Turismo.
compatibleVariants: GTS, GTS facelift, GTS Sport Design
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil feed line wear - oil starvation risk under hard driving, documented 60k+ miles.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - reduced throttle response on direct-injection V8s.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.91 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: High-output Turbo variant engine, delivering flagship performance with twin-turbo V8 power for the Sport Turismo.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure - loss of power and rough running, commonly reported 50k-80k miles.
Coolant system leaks - water pump and thermostat housing failures leading to overheating risk.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.92 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Turbo S specification V8, offering maximum naturally-aspirated-style response with twin-turbo efficiency.
commonFailures: Turbo actuator calibration drift - boost control issues and reduced performance on high-mileage units.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear under sustained high-load driving conditions.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M48.93 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement

entries.title: 2.9L PHEV
entries.history: Plug-in hybrid V6 pairing efficient electric drive with twin-turbo petrol power for the 4 E-Hybrid Sport Turismo.
commonFailures: Hybrid battery degradation - reduced electric-only range and performance, typically after 8-10 years.
ECU software compatibility issues - post-replacement coding required for hybrid system integration.

entries.title: 2.9L PHEV
entries.history: Performance plug-in hybrid V6 for the 4S E-Hybrid, balancing electric efficiency with 4S-level power delivery.
commonFailures: Hybrid battery thermal management faults - cooling system issues affecting battery longevity.
Timing chain tensioner wear - same V6 architecture concerns as non-hybrid M48.81 units.

entries.title: 4.0L PHEV
entries.history: Flagship Turbo S E-Hybrid powertrain, combining twin-turbo V8 with electric motor for ultimate Sport Turismo performance.
compatibleVariants: Turbo S E-Hybrid, Turbo S E-Hybrid Executive, Turbo S E-Hybrid facelift
commonFailures: Hybrid battery degradation - reduced electric-only range and performance, typically after 8-10 years.
ECU software compatibility issues - post-replacement coding required for hybrid system integration.

entries.history: Updated facelift PHEV V8 with refined hybrid mapping for improved efficiency and seamless power delivery.
compatibleVariants: Turbo S E-Hybrid facelift, GTS Hybrid facelift
commonFailures: Hybrid system software calibration - requires specialist Porsche PIWIS coding after engine replacement.

commonProblems.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo's M48 engine family offers refined performance, but timing chain tensioner wear on V6 units and turbocharger issues on high-mileage V8s dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo M48.80/M48.81 V6 Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4, 4S, 4 E-Hybrid, 4S E-Hybrid (2017-2024, M48.80, M48.81, M48.82, M48.83)
problems.rootCause: Early-production tensioner design allows gradual wear, leading to chain slack, rattling noise and potential catastrophic engine damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain before failure; requires engine front cover removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch causes valve timing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M48.80/M48.81 with upgraded tensioner kit, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once rattling is audible; prevents secondary damage to valves/pistons

problems.recommendation: On a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4S worth £45,000-£65,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles offers better value than waiting for failure. If rattling is already present, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M48.81 engine prices for your Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo M48.91/M48.92 V8 Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo GTS, Turbo, Turbo S (2017-2024, M48.90, M48.91, M48.92, M48.93)
problems.rootCause: Oil feed line wear and carbon buildup on turbo vanes reduce efficiency; sustained high-load driving accelerates seal degradation and bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, refurbish or replace twin turbos; clean oil feed lines; reset ECU adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block is undamaged; requires specialist Porsche diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M48.91/M48.92 with upgraded turbo components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil starvation or debris entered the engine

problems.recommendation: For Panamera Sport Turismo Turbo models valued above £70,000, a specialist turbo rebuild is cost-effective if caught early. If boost loss is accompanied by metallic noise or smoke, assume internal damage and opt for a rebuilt engine with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M48.92 Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo V8 replacement engines

problems.h4: Hybrid Battery Degradation - Why Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo E-Hybrid Models Lose Electric Range
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4 E-Hybrid, 4S E-Hybrid, Turbo S E-Hybrid (2017-2024, M48.82, M48.83, M48.94, M48.95)
problems.rootCause: Lithium-ion battery cells degrade with charge cycles and thermal stress; cooling system faults accelerate capacity loss and trigger hybrid system warnings.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Battery Health Check & Cooling Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, coolant flush for battery thermal system, software update
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early warning signs; does not restore lost capacity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace degraded battery modules, recalibrate BMS, test electric motor integration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only partial cell failure; requires Porsche PIWIS coding expertise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M48.94/M48.95 with new or reconditioned battery pack, full system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage E-Hybrids or when multiple systems show faults

problems.recommendation: E-Hybrid owners should budget for battery health checks from year 6 onwards. If electric range drops below 10 miles or hybrid warnings appear, get a specialist diagnostic before committing to costly module replacement.
problems.cta: Enquire about Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo E-Hybrid engine and battery replacement options

problems.h4: Coolant System Leaks - Why Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo V8 Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo GTS, Turbo, Turbo S (2017-2024, M48.90, M48.91, M48.92, M48.93)
problems.rootCause: Plastic thermostat housing and water pump impellers become brittle with heat cycles; leaks onto hot components cause steam, overheating and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat housing, hoses; pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable at first sign of leak; prevents secondary overheating damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M48.90/M48.92 with upgraded cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if overheating caused warped heads or blown head gasket

problems.recommendation: Address coolant leaks immediately on Panamera Sport Turismo V8 models. If temperature warnings appeared or steam was visible, assume internal damage and compare rebuilt engine quotes before proceeding with minor repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo V8 rebuilt engine prices with upgraded cooling components

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Why Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4S, GTS, Turbo (2017-2024, M48.81, M48.91, M48.92)
problems.rootCause: HPFP internal components wear under high-pressure operation; metal debris contaminates fuel rails and injectors, causing misfires, rough running and potential engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, flush fuel rails, clean or replace injectors, reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught before debris causes cylinder damage; requires specialist diagnostics

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt M48.81/M48.92 with new HPFP and fuel system components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires persisted or metallic debris found in oil/fuel system

problems.recommendation: If your Panamera Sport Turismo develops rough running or loss of power, get a fuel pressure diagnostic immediately. Early HPFP replacement is cost-effective; delayed action risks expensive engine damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement with new HPFP included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain rattles, turbo smoke, hybrid warnings or coolant leaks - these common Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine problems don't have to mean the end of your car. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components to address the original failure point and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road with confidence, without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help identify your exact replacement option.

groups.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 2.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.9L twin-turbo V6 forms the backbone of the Panamera Sport Turismo range, powering 4 and 4S variants with refined performance and efficient packaging. Introduced with the 971 generation in 2017, it remains in updated form through the 2021+ facelift.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear on early units; turbo oil seal degradation at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 2.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L twin-turbo V8 delivers flagship performance for GTS, Turbo and Turbo S Sport Turismo variants. Featuring advanced thermal management and direct injection, it offers strong torque delivery suited to the shooting brake's grand touring character.
commonFailurePoints: Coolant system leaks; high-pressure fuel pump wear; turbocharger oil feed issues
items.cta: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 2.9L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Plug-in hybrid variants pair the 2.9L V6 with an electric motor for enhanced efficiency and instant torque. The M48.82 and M48.83 codes denote 4 E-Hybrid and 4S E-Hybrid models, offering electric-only range for urban driving.
commonFailurePoints: Hybrid battery thermal management faults; ECU coding complexity post-replacement
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 2.9L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 4.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The flagship Turbo S E-Hybrid combines the 4.0L V8 with a high-output electric motor for 680hp combined. M48.94 and M48.95 codes cover pre-facelift and updated facelift versions, requiring specialist hybrid system expertise for replacement.

commonFailurePoints: Hybrid battery thermal management faults; ECU coding complexity post-replacement

commonFailurePoints: Hybrid battery degradation after 8-10 years; complex cooling circuit maintenance

groups.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo 2.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.9L twin-turbo diesel V6 was offered exclusively in the 4S Diesel variant for the early 971 generation. Discontinued after 2018 due to shifting market demand, it remains a sought-after option for high-mileage touring.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging; DPF regeneration issues on short-journey usage

engineYears.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo's production timeline (2017-present), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Panamera Sport Turismo launched with the new M48 engine family, introducing twin-turbo V6 and V8 units across petrol and diesel variants.
keyChanges: Panamera Sport Turismo body style introduced alongside saloon range
M48.80/M48.81 2.9L V6 twin-turbo petrol engines debut
M48.90/M48.92 4.0L V8 twin-turbo petrol engines for GTS/Turbo
M48.60 2.9L V6 twin-turbo diesel offered in 4S Diesel variant

knownFor: Early-production M48.80/M48.81 timing chain tensioner wear reports
Strong initial demand for Sport Turismo shooting brake practicality
PHEV variants praised for seamless electric/petrol transition

knownFor: Timing chain concerns remain top search query for M48.81

PHEV battery degradation queries increase on 2017-2018 models

faq.h2: Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £4,200 for a 2.9L V6 to £15,000 for a Turbo S E-Hybrid unit, supply only. Supply & fit adds £1,200-£3,500 depending on variant complexity.
keyPoints: 2.9L V6 rebuilt: £4,200-£8,500 supply only
4.0L V8 rebuilt: £6,500-£12,500 supply only
PHEV variants: £8,500-£15,000 due to hybrid system complexity
Supply & fit labour typically adds £1,200-£3,500
items.cta: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement quotes today

items.question: What is the most reliable Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine code?
items.answer: Updated M48.81 (2021+ facelift) and M48.91 GTS units show fewer early failure reports than pre-facelift codes, though all M48 engines benefit from specialist maintenance.
keyPoints: Facelift M48.81 features revised timing chain tensioner design
M48.91 GTS benefits from robust V8 architecture with regular servicing
PHEV codes require hybrid system expertise for long-term reliability
Regular oil changes with Porsche-approved specs critical for all variants
items.cta: Find rebuilt Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engines with upgraded reliability components

items.question: Can I replace my Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine with a different size?
items.answer: Engine swaps between displacements are technically complex and may affect MOT compliance, insurance validity and ECU coding; sticking to the original engine code is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: M48 family shares architecture but ECU coding is variant-specific
Changing displacement may require emissions re-certification for MOT
Insurance providers often require declaration of non-standard engine fitment
Specialist Porsche coding equipment needed for any cross-variant installation
items.cta: Get quotes for your exact Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine code

items.question: How long does a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days; supply & fit installations require 3-7 days depending on variant complexity and garage workload.
keyPoints: Standard V6/V8 replacements: 3-4 days for supply & fit
PHEV variants: 5-7 days due to hybrid system calibration requirements
Pre-ordering rebuilt engines can reduce waiting time
Post-installation road test and diagnostics included in specialist fitment
items.cta: Book Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine supply & fit with confirmed timeline

items.question: What warranty comes with a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty; some specialists offer extended coverage up to 3 years for rebuilt units.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Rebuilt/remanufactured units often eligible for 24-36 month extensions
Warranty covers internal components; exclude external items like turbos unless specified
Always confirm exact terms, claim process and approved fitting centres before purchase
items.cta: Compare Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How do I find my Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code appears on the VIN plate in the driver's door shut, in the service book, or can be instantly verified by entering your registration number on EngineMarket.
keyPoints: Check VIN plate in driver's door shut for M48.xX code
Service invoices and MOT certificates often list engine code
Porsche PIWIS diagnostics can read engine code via OBD port
EngineMarket's registration lookup instantly identifies your exact code
items.cta: Use EngineMarket's free registration check to identify your Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine code

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine?
items.answer: For Sport Turismo models valued above £35,000, a rebuilt engine with warranty typically offers better value than part-exchange or scrappage, preserving the premium shooting brake's utility and performance.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost vs vehicle value is key decision factor
Sport Turismo body style retains strong residual demand in UK market
Warranty-covered rebuild protects against repeat failure costs
Specialist fitting maintains Porsche resale value better than non-specialist work
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to assess if engine replacement makes financial sense for your Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo

items.question: Can I fit a Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine myself?
items.answer: M48 engine replacement requires specialist Porsche tools, PIWIS coding equipment and hybrid system expertise for PHEV variants; professional fitting is strongly recommended to protect warranty and vehicle electronics.
keyPoints: M48 engines require precise torque specs and alignment procedures
ECU adaptation reset and coding needs Porsche PIWIS diagnostic tool
PHEV variants demand high-voltage safety protocols and hybrid calibration
Incorrect installation can void engine warranty and cause secondary damage
items.cta: Find accredited Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and used Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines undergo comprehensive refurbishment with upgraded components addressing known M48 failure points, while used engines are donor units sold as-is with variable mileage and limited warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: new bearings, seals, tensioners; bench-tested; 12-month warranty
Used: mileage-dependent; compression test recommended; 3-6 month typical warranty
Rebuilt offers predictable longevity; used offers lower upfront cost
PHEV used engines require hybrid battery health verification before purchase
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine options with warranty details

items.question: Do Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo replacement engines come with ECU?
items.answer: Most replacement engines are supplied without ECU; your original ECU is typically reused after specialist coding to match the rebuilt engine's serial number and calibration data.
keyPoints: Original ECU retained and recoded to match rebuilt engine serial number
PIWIS coding required for immobiliser sync and adaptation reset
PHEV variants need additional hybrid control module calibration
Specialist suppliers can advise on ECU-inclusive options where available
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Panamera Sport Turismo engine replacement with ECU coding included
